core.action: Enforce strict temporal ordering invariant: prior_G timestamps must be strictly less than batch_G timestamps, and batch_G timestamps must be less than or equal to cumulative_G timestamps — validate ordering before each training iteration and fail-fast if violated

consequence.description: Violating temporal ordering causes future events to leak into past predictions — model learns to predict historical events using future information, making predictions meaningless for financial forecasting applications

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-133

core.when: When implementing or refactoring provider/credential management in the data loading pipeline
core.action: Assume that provider priority and credential isolation are handled by default framework behavior — the framework does not implement provider priority or credential isolation mechanisms

consequence.description: Without provider priority and credential isolation, requests may be routed to the wrong provider, credentials may leak across provider boundaries, causing production failures and potential security incidents

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-004

core.when: When configuring provider settings in the data loading pipeline
core.action: Implement explicit provider priority ordering (e.g., priority field in provider config) and credential isolation (e.g., separate credential stores per provider using provider_id as namespace key)

consequence.description: Without explicit provider priority and isolated credentials, production reliability is at risk due to ambiguous routing and potential credential cross-contamination between providers

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-004

core.when: When implementing temporal feature encoding in the neural network training pipeline
core.action: Compute time_interval as the minimum difference between unique timestamps in the dataset, using this as the fundamental time unit for temporal encoding normalization

consequence.description: Using mean, median, or fixed time intervals instead of minimum timestamp difference causes temporal features to be on inconsistent scales across datasets, leading to numerical instability during neural network training where gradient magnitudes become unpredictable

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-003

validation_threshold: time_interval == 0 OR time_interval is not computed from min(diff(unique_timestamps)) → FAIL

core.when: When implementing temporal feature engineering for inter-event time calculations
core.action: Verify that time_interval matches the fundamental time unit of the dataset by checking min(diff(unique_timestamps)) against known data granularity (e.g., tick data=seconds, daily data=days)

consequence.description: If time_interval is not aligned with actual data granularity, neural network temporal features will have undefined scales causing training divergence or poor convergence, with errors accumulating silently across epochs

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-003

core.when: When processing dynamic knowledge graph data for link prediction or event forecasting
core.action: Assume the framework provides point-in-time data availability checking — the framework does not implement PIT data validation; raw data may include information not available at prediction time

consequence.description: Without point-in-time data validation, backtested strategy performance includes look-ahead bias where predictions use future information not available at signal generation time, making live trading results fall far below backtested performance

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-002

core.when: When loading historical data for model training or evaluation in dynamic knowledge graph forecasting
core.action: Implement point-in-time validation by comparing each data point's timestamp against the model's knowledge cutoff, ensuring only information available before prediction time is used for feature computation

consequence.description: Without PIT data filtering, the model ingests future information during training leading to overfitted results that cannot replicate in live trading where only historical data is available at each decision point

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-GAP-002

core.when: When configuring training pipeline for link prediction and temporal point process models
core.action: Instantiate separate optimizer instances for edge prediction and time prediction tasks with independently configurable learning rates, rather than sharing a single optimizer across both tasks

consequence.description: Using a shared optimizer forces edge and time predictions to compete for gradient updates with incompatible learning rate requirements, causing one task to dominate training while the other fails to converge properly

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-027

validation_threshold: shared_optimizer_used == True → FAIL; edge_optimizer is None OR time_optimizer is None → FAIL

core.when: When setting up loss weight optimization for multi-task learning in dynamic knowledge graphs
core.action: Perform grid search over edge/time loss weight combinations on validation data rather than using fixed manual weights, ensuring optimal task balance for each specific dataset

consequence.description: Fixed loss weights cause suboptimal task balance where one prediction task dominates gradient updates, leading to models that excel at one task but fail at the other, reducing overall prediction quality

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-033

validation_threshold: loss_weights is not grid_search_result → WARN; loss_weights == fixed_value → WARN

core.when: When configuring grid search for automatic loss weight discovery
core.action: Define explicit grid search ranges with minimum 3 candidate values per task weight, and use nested cross-validation or held-out validation set to prevent overfitting loss weights to validation noise

consequence.description: Unbounded or narrowly defined grid search causes loss weights to overfit validation set noise, producing weights that maximize validation metrics but generalize poorly to test data

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-033

core.when: When implementing embedding update mechanisms in dynamic knowledge graph models
core.action: Use separate embedding spaces and update mechanisms for structural patterns (who connects to whom) and temporal patterns (when events occur), with distinct GraphStructuralRNNConv and GraphTemporalRNNConv modules

consequence.description: Joint embeddings force structural and temporal dynamics to compete in the same representation space, causing persistent structural relationships to be overwritten by transient temporal patterns and reducing link prediction accuracy

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-004

validation_threshold: shared_embedding_space == True → FAIL; structural_update mechanism == temporal_update mechanism → WARN

core.when: When configuring the temporal mixture model for financial event prediction
core.action: Verify that 128 mixture components are sufficient for the diversity of temporal patterns in the data - for datasets with many distinct event types (earnings, regulatory, macro), increase to 256-512 components; for simpler datasets, 64 may suffice

consequence.description: Default 128 mixture components may be insufficient for datasets with high temporal pattern diversity, causing distinct patterns to merge and reducing prediction accuracy for rare but important financial events like earnings surprises or regulatory filings

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-022

core.when: When configuring knowledge graph link prediction loss weights
core.action: Change the default loss weighting scheme (tail=1.0, rel=0.2, head=0.1) without empirical validation - the weighting reflects task priorities where tail prediction is primary

consequence.description: Modifying loss weights without validation causes the model to overfit to easier prediction tasks (head prediction) while underfitting to critical tail entity predictions, resulting in incomplete or incorrect knowledge graph completion that propagates errors to downstream trading decisions

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-018

core.when: When implementing loss computation for multi-component training objectives
core.action: Verify that loss component scales are comparable before using equal weighting via sum(dict.values()) — inspect raw loss values for temporal, edge, and relation components and apply normalization if scales differ by more than 10x

consequence.description: Equal weighting of loss components without scale verification causes the optimizer to privilege high-magnitude components while under-optimizing others, leading to biased representations where some objectives (e.g., temporal vs relation prediction) are systematically neglected during training

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-122

core.when: When implementing or verifying graph convolution layers in the transformer architecture
core.action: Assume the model architecture defaults provide optimal GCN depth — explicitly verify that num_gconv_layers is set to 2 (the validated configuration for second-order neighborhood capture without over-smoothing)

consequence.description: Using incorrect GCN depth (single layer misses second-order neighbors; 3+ layers risks over-smoothing) causes node representations to either lack sufficient receptive field or become indistinguishable, degrading edge prediction accuracy and ranking performance

evidence_refs.summary: Derived from BD: BD-108

core.when: When implementing or modifying temporal prediction model evaluation logic
core.action: Use log_prob_density method for continuous-time evaluation metrics; use log_prob_interval method for discrete-time training — these methods MUST NOT be merged or substituted for each other

consequence.description: Using interval-based evaluation on continuous-time metrics produces systematically biased results that cannot be compared with other temporal models, leading to incorrect strategy selection based on flawed evaluation metrics

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-025

validation_threshold: training_uses_density == True OR evaluation_uses_interval == True → FAIL

core.when: When implementing the RGCN degree normalization computation
core.action: Pre-compute degree normalization once before training and reuse across each GNN layers — degree normalization MUST NOT be recomputed on every forward pass

consequence.description: Recomputing degree normalization on every forward pass wastes computational resources on invariant values without improving model quality, causing unnecessary training time inflation

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-037

validation_threshold: comp_deg_norm not in module scope OR computed inside forward pass → FAIL

core.when: When implementing knowledge graph convolution layers
core.action: Use relation-aware attention with separate key/value projections per relation type — attention computation MUST NOT be relation-agnostic

consequence.description: Relation-agnostic attention treats all edges equivalently, losing critical relational semantics in financial KGs where 'acquires' and 'mentions' relations carry fundamentally different predictive weight, causing the model to learn suboptimal attention patterns

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-009

core.when: When implementing temporal graph convolution for financial entity updates
core.action: Apply edge normalization with time decay factor during temporal convolution — distant interactions MUST contribute proportionally less than recent ones

consequence.description: Without time decay normalization, stale and recent financial interactions are treated equally, reducing temporal sensitivity and causing entity states to be contaminated by outdated information that no longer reflects market conditions

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-012

validation_threshold: edge_norm == None OR uniform_weights == True → FAIL

core.when: When configuring training regularization hyperparameters
core.action: Use weight_decay=0.00001 (1e-5) for this architecture — verify that higher values are not applied without explicit justification, as this architecture relies on multiple regularization mechanisms (early stopping, model capacity limits) that work synergistically with this conservative weight decay

consequence.description: Increasing weight decay to 0.001 or 0.01 over-regularizes the model, impairing capacity needed for complex financial patterns and degrading strategy performance below expected backtest levels

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-041

core.when: When implementing context construction for temporal prediction in DKG.model.tpp
core.action: Concatenate source/destination embeddings with relation and dynamic relation embeddings to preserve entity identity information required for entity-specific timing patterns

consequence.description: Replacing concatenation with average pooling loses entity identity information, breaking entity-specific timing patterns and causing incorrect temporal predictions for different entity pairs

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-094

core.when: When implementing optimizer configuration for embedding model training in DKG.train
core.action: Use AdamW with decoupled weight decay — do not substitute standard Adam with L2 regularization or SGD with momentum for embedding model training

consequence.description: Standard Adam with L2 regularization interacts improperly with adaptive learning rate scheduling, causing degraded generalization in embedding models and lower quality entity representations

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-097

core.when: When implementing gradient clipping for RNN training in DKG.utils.train_utils
core.action: Use gradient-preserving clipping that clamps large gradients while maintaining gradient direction information — do not substitute hard clipping that discards directional signal

consequence.description: Hard clipping loses gradient direction information that RNNs rely on for stable training, causing unstable gradients and degraded temporal modeling performance

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-098

core.when: When implementing message passing for RGCN in DKG.utils.model_utils
core.action: Normalize incoming messages by in-degree using comp_deg_norm function to prevent high-degree hub nodes from dominating aggregated representations — verify zero-handling for nodes without incoming edges

consequence.description: Omitting in-degree normalization causes high-degree hub nodes to dominate aggregated node representations, leading to representation collapse and degraded graph modeling performance

evidence_refs.summary: bd75205 Derived from BD: BD-099

core.when: When implementing RNN training for temporal modeling in DKG.train
core.action: Use truncated backpropagation through time (BPTT) with fixed truncation length N to bound memory usage — do not use full BPTT for long sequences

consequence.description: Full BPTT grows memory linearly with sequence length, making training on long temporal histories computationally infeasible and causing out-of-memory errors
